verdict_reasoning: FN1 encodes fibronectin, a secreted glycoprotein present in soluble form in plasma and deposited in the extracellular matrix and at cell surfaces. It binds to cell-surface integrins and other ECM components via non-covalent interactions but has no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or other membrane-anchoring mechanism of its own. It reaches the cell surface only through recruitment by integrin binding, not via its own intrinsic membrane anchor. Extracellular ligands without self-directed surface anchoring fall under the secreted_only category.
